text: The enormous height of a hundred and twenty cubits is given in the Hebrew text, but the manuscripts of the Septuagint Greek seem doubtful about the inclusion of the ‘hundred’. The temple itself was only thirty cubits high (III Kg. 6.2).

The words enclosed in brackets are obviously meaningless. Some think that, by a very slight error, the Hebrew word for the ‘inner shrine’ has been written by a mistake for a word meaning ‘a necklace’.
